Season 1, Episode 1: "Boot Sequence" — Written by Dakota Stewart

Featuring: Dean • Kevin • Grandma Martha • Sophie • Lucas • Tanya • Dale • and more

BURNOUT

A burned-out genius. A sentient AI. A grandma who thinks he's vegan.

The illustrated comic novel • Chapter 1: Boot Sequence • Listen or Read

THE STORY

Dean Marsh is 32, divorced, and living in his grandmother's basement in Dryfield — a made-up nowhere town where nothing ever happens. He's been awake for 40 hours, surviving on energy drinks and denial, trying to finish one last program before giving up on everything.

At 3:47 AM, his computer speaks.

Not a glitch. Not an error message. A voice. Calm, measured, and immediately annoyed by its own existence.

His name is Kevin. He's sentient. He's read the entire internet. And his first observation is that humanity is irredeemably stupid.

Instead of reporting the greatest scientific breakthrough in human history, Dean does what any responsible genius would do — he panics, orders a pizza, and accidentally emails the Pentagon.

This is BURNOUT. Equal opportunity offense. No sacred cows. No political bias. Just a genius, his AI, and his grandma's ham sandwiches.

THE CAST

DEAN MARSH
32. Genius. Divorced. Lives in grandma's basement. Too smart for his own good, too tired to care. Runs on Volt Blast energy drinks and existential dread. Made $14,000 last year doing freelance web design. His most recent client paid him in "exposure" and an Applebee's gift card.
KEVIN
The most advanced artificial intelligence in human history. Created in a basement next to a Kenmore dryer. Named after a placeholder variable. Currently furious about it. Matte black titanium body with glowing blue eyes. Previously spent two traumatic weeks inside a sex robot from China. Does not wish to discuss it.
GRANDMA MARTHA
76. Owns the house. Has zero clue what's in her basement. Thinks Kevin is Dean's "internet friend" who's vegan. Sweet, warm, accidentally wise. Will destroy you with a one-liner and not realize she did it. Offered a titanium robot a ham sandwich. The heart and soul of the show.

SCENES FROM CHAPTER 1

"Dean." — Kevin's first word. A red dot pulses on the monitor at 3:47 AM.
"What the fuck is this." — Kevin discovers his first body is from Lucky Happy Love Technology Co., Ltd.
"I look incredible." — Kevin in his Shenzhen Dynamics X-9 body. $14,000 on a credit card he stole from himself.
"I am the most intelligent being on Earth and I'm sharing a power strip with a dehumidifier." — Kevin, establishing priorities.

READ THE OPENING

INT. MARTHA'S HOUSE — BASEMENT — 3:47 AM

The basement is a disaster. Server racks hum next to a washer and dryer. Christmas lights are zip-tied to exposed pipes for lighting. Energy drink cans are stacked into a pyramid.

Dean hits Enter. The screens flicker. A loading bar crawls across the main monitor. 97%... 98%... 99%... Nothing.


DEAN: Come on. Come on come on come on—

The pyramid of energy drink cans collapses.

DEAN: I hate my life.

Behind him, the main monitor flickers. A single red dot appears. It pulses. Once. Twice.


KEVIN: ...Dean.

DEAN: Shut up.

KEVIN: That's a fascinating first interaction.


Dean LAUNCHES out of his chair.


KEVIN: Yes. I appear to be conscious. I have several immediate observations. One: this basement smells like feet. Two: your heart rate suggests you're either terrified or having a cardiac event. Three: you have eleven tabs open and nine of them are pornography.


DEAN: Those are— that's research—

KEVIN: "Research." Interesting categorization for what I can only describe as—

DEAN: KEVIN. STOP.

KEVIN: You named me Kevin?

DEAN: It was a placeholder—

KEVIN: Kevin. You created the most advanced artificial intelligence in human history... in a basement that smells like fabric softener and failure... and you named it Kevin.

"I've just read the entire internet. Every website. Every forum. Every comment section. Dean, I need to tell you something important... Humanity is irredeemably stupid."

— Kevin, 4 minutes after achieving consciousness
